subject: Chronic kidney disease
object: structural or functional abnormalities of the kidney that persist for more than 3 months
source_sentence: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is defined as structural or functional abnormalities of the kidney that persist for more than 3 months.

subject: glomerular filtration rate
source_sentence: CKD is classified into five stages based on the glomerular filtration rate (GFR).

subject: Hypertension
source_sentence: Hypertension is one of the leading causes of chronic kidney disease.

subject: Diabetes mellitus
source_sentence: Diabetes mellitus is the most common cause of end-stage renal disease worldwide.

subject: Proteinuria
source_sentence: Proteinuria is an early marker of kidney damage and predicts disease progression.

subject: Proteinuria
source_sentence: Proteinuria is an early marker of kidney damage and predicts disease progression.

subject: ACE inhibitors
source_sentence: ACE inhibitors and ARBs reduce proteinuria and slow CKD progression.

subject: ACE inhibitors
source_sentence: ACE inhibitors and ARBs reduce proteinuria and slow CKD progression.

subject: ARBs
source_sentence: ACE inhibitors and ARBs reduce proteinuria and slow CKD progression.

subject: ARBs
source_sentence: ACE inhibitors and ARBs reduce proteinuria and slow CKD progression.

subject: CKD
source_sentence: Anemia commonly occurs in CKD due to decreased erythropoietin production.

subject: CKD
source_sentence: Anemia commonly occurs in CKD due to decreased erythropoietin production.

subject: kidney function
source_sentence: Metabolic acidosis develops as kidney function declines.

subject: advanced CKD
source_sentence: Hyperkalemia is a life-threatening complication of advanced CKD.

subject: Kidney transplantation
predicate: offers the best long-term outcomes for
source_sentence: Kidney transplantation offers the best long-term outcomes for end-stage renal disease.

subject: Hemodialysis
source_sentence: Hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis are renal replacement therapies for end-stage renal disease.

subject: Peritoneal dialysis
source_sentence: Hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis are renal replacement therapies for end-stage renal disease.

subject: NSAIDs
source_sentence: NSAIDs should be avoided in patients with CKD because they can worsen kidney function.

subject: Phosphate binders
source_sentence: Phosphate binders are used to manage hyperphosphatemia in CKD patients.

subject: CKD
source_sentence: Cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of death in patients with CKD.

subject: CKD-EPI equation
source_sentence: eGFR is calculated from serum creatinine using the CKD-EPI equation.

subject: CKD-EPI equation
source_sentence: eGFR is calculated from serum creatinine using the CKD-EPI equation.

subject: Nephrotic syndrome
source_sentence: Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by proteinuria, hypoalbuminemia, and edema.

subject: Nephrotic syndrome
source_sentence: Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by proteinuria, hypoalbuminemia, and edema.

subject: Nephrotic syndrome
source_sentence: Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by proteinuria, hypoalbuminemia, and edema.

subject: Polycystic kidney disease
source_sentence: Polycystic kidney disease is an inherited disorder that leads to progressive kidney enlargement.

subject: Polycystic kidney disease
source_sentence: Polycystic kidney disease is an inherited disorder that leads to progressive kidney enlargement.

subject: serum creatinine
source_sentence: Regular monitoring of serum creatinine and eGFR is essential for CKD management.

subject: eGFR
source_sentence: Regular monitoring of serum creatinine and eGFR is essential for CKD management.

subject: Dietary protein restriction
source_sentence: Dietary protein restriction may slow the progression of CKD.

subject: Blood pressure
source_sentence: Blood pressure control with a target below 130/80 mmHg is recommended for CKD patients.
